title: A Genius With Amnesia - Victor Savkin, Nx
summary: Victor Savkin argues that current coding agents suffer from severe limitations: they can only view small portions of codebases and lose context between interactions, making them ineffective despite individual competence. He frames this as analogous to a master engineer with amnesia and proposes that solutions like Nx and Polygraph can address these constraints.
writeup: Victor Savkin of Nx makes the case that today's coding agents are individually competent but hobbled by two structural limits: they see only small portions of a codebase, and they lose context between interactions. He frames this as a master engineer with amnesia, capable on any single task yet unable to retain a working picture of the larger system. The talk presents Nx and Polygraph as approaches to these constraints, connecting the problem to context windows, agent memory, and monorepo structure.
